name: claude-hooks-ups-pipeline
tags: pipeline, graph, UPS, UserPromptSubmit, langgraph, nodes, fan-out, parallel, session graph, topology, active task, task context, task context creation, context injection, tier1, tier2, how task context works, cache_check, prompt cache, short-circuit, summarize_task_context
body:
load_turn → cache_check runs first (epic c0f3037f). On a cache hit, cache_check short-circuits straight to set_prompt_id, skipping the entire task/memory/tool-hint/RAG fan-out below — the cache is a quick-answer lookup, not task context, so there's no reason to pay for that work.

On a cache miss (the common case), the graph proceeds as before:

When a task is active, UPS builds task context by running 4 nodes in parallel (tier 1): load_task_history, load_task_code, load_related_tasks, load_related_commits. (load_active_review was removed June 2026 when the review system was removed.)

As of task:b9dded3e (2026-07-04), tier 1 no longer fans straight into tier 2 — it fans into summarize_task_context first, which compresses task_context/rag_chunks/related_* into task_context_summary (first-turn-of-active-task gated; a fast pass-through on every other turn), then fans out to tier 2.

Two parallel fan-out tiers:
Tier 1 (task context nodes): load_active_task → [load_task_history ∥ load_task_code ∥ load_related_tasks ∥ load_related_commits] → summarize_task_context (fan-in then single node)
Tier 2 (context nodes): summarize_task_context → [cwd_domain_detect ∥ load_memories ∥ score_tools] → set_prompt_id → log_task_events → END
Without an active task, all tier-1 nodes return empty dicts and tier-2 runs context-only.

Cross-repo note: load_task_code.py and load_related_commits.py both resolve their repo-scoped index from state["cwd"] first, falling back to a hardcoded default repo (claude-hooks) only if no index exists at cwd — see [[cwd-first-fallback-default-repo-pattern]]. load_related_commits.py was fixed to follow this pattern in task:4b7ccfe8 (2026-07-02); before that it always queried claude-hooks' diff index regardless of the active task's actual repo.
